dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My name is Song Wooyeon. It's a perfectly ordinary name for a perfectly ordinary girl.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My older brother, Song Geunwoo, is a genius law student, already mapping out his future with laser precision. My younger sister, Song Woojoo, is a talented dancer, graceful and confident, always in the spotlight.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And me? I'm just... me. The middle child. The one who blends into the wallpaper. The invisible one.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Every morning, it's the same routine. Wake up, get ready, try not to disturb anyone. Try not to make a sound.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My mother's voice, always so clear, always so pointed.

dialogue.speaker: Mother (thought)
dialogue.line: "Wooyeon, try to be more like your brother. Or your sister." I just nod. What else can I do?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Today, it was another errand. A trip to the stationery store for Woojoo's special art supplies. Another task for the invisible girl.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: FOOTSTEPS (soft)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: As I walked through the park, my head down, trying to avoid eye contact with anyone, a strange sight caught my eye.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A group of people. Not just any people. They were... colorful. Vibrant. Like a splash of paint on a muted canvas.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: One guy, in particular, stood out. Bright, fiery red hair that seemed to defy gravity. A loud, patterned shirt that screamed for attention. He was posing like a statue, one arm dramatically extended.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: What in the world are they doing?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Around him, others were sketching furiously. A girl with bright pink hair, meticulously shading her drawing. A guy with glasses and a beanie, his brow furrowed in concentration. Another, with a stern expression, but a surprisingly gentle hand on his sketchbook.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: SCRATCH (of pencils)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: They weren't just drawing; they were *living* in that moment. Laughing, chatting, completely absorbed in their world. So unbothered by the curious stares, the passersby.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was so different from my world, where every movement felt measured, every word carefully chosen to avoid drawing attention. To avoid being judged.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: And the red-haired guy... he had these incredibly long, slender fingers. Moving with such grace, even when he wasn't drawing. They almost seemed to... dance.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: He looked so confident. So unselfconscious. So... unlike me.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I must have been staring. My heart pounded, a frantic drum against my ribs.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUMP-THUMP (heartbeat)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Suddenly, the red-haired guy's eyes flickered in my direction. Just for a second. A quick, piercing glance that seemed to see right through me.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: GASPE (Wooyeon)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I quickly looked away, my cheeks burning, clutching the bag of art supplies tighter. He didn't seem to notice. Or maybe he just didn't care.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RUSTLE (bag)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I hurried away, my cheeks still hot. It was embarrassing to be caught staring. To be caught at all.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: But even as I walked, I couldn't shake the image of them. The colors, the laughter, the sheer uninhibited joy. The way they just... existed.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: I clutched my bag, the weight of Woojoo's art supplies a strange irony. I was buying tools for someone else's creativity, while mine remained locked away, unexplored.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: What would it be like to be so bold? To wear your personality on your sleeve, like that red-haired guy? To be part of something so vibrant, so unapologetically *them*?

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: My life felt like a grayscale photograph next to their vivid painting. A dull, muted existence.

dialogue.speaker: Wooyeon
dialogue.line: The image of his 'spirit fingers' – those long, graceful digits – lingered in my mind. They seemed to hold a secret, a power I couldn't comprehend.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A tiny, almost imperceptible spark ignited within me. A flicker of something new, a quiet whisper that maybe, just maybe, I didn't have to be invisible forever.

summary: Song Wooyeon, an ordinary and self-conscious high schooler, feels overshadowed by her talented siblings and constantly tries to remain invisible. While on an errand for her sister, she stumbles upon a vibrant, eccentric art club in the park, catching a glimpse of their lively world and the captivating 'Spirit Fingers' of one of its members, sparking a tiny flicker of curiosity within her.
